title: cerastium arcticum subsp. procerum
intro: Cerastium arcticum subsp. procerum, also known as the Arctic mouse-ear or Alaskan mouse-ear, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carnation family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, and is believed to have various health benefits such as reducing fever, soothing skin irritations, and treating respiratory issues. The herbal preparations of Cerastium arcticum subsp. procerum typically involve infusions or decoctions of the leaves and flowers, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, or salves. While more research is needed to confirm its efficacy, this plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries to promote overall health and well-being.
